Association of Catholic Colleges and Universities

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Abbreviation | ACCU |
| Formation | 1899 |
| Type | Nonprofit association of colleges and universities |
| Purpose | Promote and strengthen the mission and character of Catholic higher education in the United States[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Headquarters | [Washington, D.C.](/wiki/Washington,_D.C. "Washington, D.C.") |
| Region served | [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") |
| President | Dr. Donna Carroll, Ed.D. |
| Affiliations | [IFCU](/wiki/International_Federation_of_Catholic_Universities "International Federation of Catholic Universities") |
| Website | [www.accunet.org](http://www.accunet.org) [Edit this at Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q4809686#P856 "Edit this at Wikidata") |

The **Association of Catholic Colleges and Universities** (**ACCU**) is a voluntary association of delegates from [Catholic institutions of higher learning](/wiki/Catholic_higher_education "Catholic higher education"). ACCU lists a total 230 Catholic institutions of [higher education in the United States](/wiki/Higher_education_in_the_United_States "Higher education in the United States").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Association_of_Catholic_Colleges_and_Universities&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

It was founded in 1899 by fifty-three delegates from [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") colleges across the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"). There are 247 degree-granting Catholic [postsecondary](/wiki/Postsecondary "Postsecondary") institutions in the US. Currently the association includes more than 90% of accredited Catholic institutions of higher learning in the United States as well as over twenty international universities.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The president or rector of each participating institution serves as voting representative to the ACCU. There is also collaboration with the [United States Conference of Catholic Bishops](/wiki/United_States_Conference_of_Catholic_Bishops "United States Conference of Catholic Bishops"), the [National Catholic Educational Association](/wiki/National_Catholic_Educational_Association "National Catholic Educational Association") and various other agencies.

## List of recent presidents

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Association_of_Catholic_Colleges_and_Universities&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: List of recent presidents")]

* [Monika Hellwig](/wiki/Monika_Hellwig "Monika Hellwig"), Ph.D. (1996–2005)
* Richard Yanikoski, Ph.D. (2005–2010)
* Michael Galligan-Stierle, Ph.D. (2010–2019)
* Fr. [Dennis H. Holtschneider](/wiki/Dennis_H._Holtschneider "Dennis H. Holtschneider"), [C.M.](/wiki/Congregation_of_the_Mission "Congregation of the Mission"), Ed.D. (2019–2024)
* Donna M. Carroll, Ed.D. (2024-Present)
